item.text: I’ll read the finding and inspect the referenced code paths first, then make the smallest source change that removes the vulnerability while preserving behavior.

item.text: The report points at `ConceptReferenceRangeUtility`, specifically criteria evaluation. I’m checking the implementation and tests around that class so the patch can target the injection surface without changing the supported criteria language.

The injection comes from constructing a Velocity template directly from the criteria string. I’m looking at existing tests and data fixtures now to understand the exact criteria forms the application already relies on.

item.aggregated_output:
api/src/test/resources/org/openmrs/api/include/ConceptServiceTest-conceptReferenceRange.xml
api/src/test/java/org/openmrs/util/ConceptReferenceRangeUtilityTest.java

item.text: The supported criteria are Java-like boolean expressions using Velocity property/method access (`$patient`, `$fn`, `$obs`, etc.). The remediation needs to stop arbitrary Velocity directives while keeping those expressions, so I’m checking the test file in detail before editing.

item.text: The current implementation exposes two risks: criteria can break out into Velocity directives, and Velocity method introspection can reach reflective APIs. I’m checking the bundled Velocity version/config surface so the fix uses its sandbox support where available and adds input validation around the expression itself.

item.text: I’m going to add an expression validator in front of Velocity, configure Velocity’s secure introspector, and add regression tests that prove directives/reflection-style method access are rejected while the existing expression behavior remains intact.

The code now rejects non-expression Velocity syntax and unsafe method access before evaluation, and Velocity is initialized with the secure uberspector. I’ve added focused regression tests and am running the utility test class to catch compatibility issues.

The formatting issue is fixed. I’m running the focused Maven test with the installed Maven now.

item.text: The focused test run is blocked by dependency resolution: Maven cannot resolve several BOMs because `mavenrepo.openmrs.org` is not reachable from this environment. I’m still doing a local sanity pass on the patch and tightening a couple of details that don’t require dependency downloads.

item.text: Offline Maven also fails because the required BOMs are not cached locally. One more local hardening pass found that Velocity’s quiet-reference syntax (`$!foo`) should be rejected too, so I’m adding explicit reference-token validation before wrapping up.
I’m adding a small regression case for quiet references as well, then I’ll run the non-network checks again.

 M api/src/test/java/org/openmrs/util/ConceptReferenceRangeUtilityTest.java

item.text: I’m loosening the validator slightly to avoid narrowing the criteria language more than necessary. It will still block directives, reflection/classloader/runtime paths, quiet references, and mutating method prefixes, while leaving ordinary predicate methods available.

I’m running the final non-network checks and one last Maven attempt so the verification status is explicit.

item.text:
Implemented the remediation in [ConceptReferenceRangeUtility.java](/work/api/src/main/java/org/openmrs/util/ConceptReferenceRangeUtility.java:116).

What changed:
- Added pre-validation for criteria to reject Velocity directives, quiet references, unknown roots, unsafe reflection/runtime members, and mutating method prefixes.
- Configured Velocity with `SecureUberspector`.
- Preserved normal criteria expressions using `$patient`, `$fn`, `$context`, `$obs`, `$encounter`, and `$date`.
- Added regression tests in [ConceptReferenceRangeUtilityTest.java](/work/api/src/test/java/org/openmrs/util/ConceptReferenceRangeUtilityTest.java:125) for directive injection, reflection access, quiet references, and mutating method calls.

Verification:
- `git diff --check` passes.
- `mvn -pl api -Dtest=ConceptReferenceRangeUtilityTest test` could not run because Maven cannot resolve BOM dependencies due DNS/network failure for `mavenrepo.openmrs.org`.
